# v0.4 routing gates TDD evidence

## Source and user journeys

No external plan file was used. The journeys came from the observed failure where a Luna-class task was asked to resolve cross-module contracts and a stopped `changes_requested` task continued to look like active rework.

- As a controller, I want registration to reject unresolved or untestable work so that model upgrades do not hide a bad task boundary.
- As a controller, I want failed review separated from actual execution so that heartbeat and sidebar state do not claim a stopped worker is reworking.
- As a controller, I want bounded retry and reclaim transitions so that low-cost workers cannot loop on judgment-heavy work.
- As an existing v0.3 user, I want old `返工` records migrated safely to `待决`.
- As a controller, I want `workClass` bound to the exact GPT-5.6 worker model so that an old `gpt-5.4-mini` slug cannot pass as merely economical.
- As an installer, I want a read-only report of active legacy registrations so that remediation preserves their audit identity.

## RED and GREEN evidence

| Stage | Command | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| RED | `npm test` | Expected failure | Missing `controllerDispatchRework` export and old title remained `返工｜01 ...` instead of `待决｜01 ...`. |
| Model-binding RED | `npm test` | Expected failure | Missing `auditModelRouting` export demonstrated that old model slugs were not rejected or discoverable. |
| GREEN | `npm test` | PASS | 31 tests passed, 0 failed. |
| Syntax | `npm run check` | PASS | Node syntax check completed successfully. |
| Skill validation | `PYTHONUTF8=1 py -3.11 .../quick_validate.py skill/codex-task-control` | PASS | `Skill is valid!` |

## Test specification

| # | What is guaranteed | Test target | Test type | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Registration rejects unresolved decisions, controller-only work, and missing scope/acceptance/boundaries | `fails closed unless delegated work is decision-complete and testable` | Integration | PASS |
| 2 | `changes_requested` stops the worker and appears in `routingQueue`, not `activeTasks` | `changes requested enters a stopped routing queue instead of pretending to run` | Unit | PASS |
| 3 | Judgment failures cannot be redispatched and can be reclaimed as an archiveable terminal task | `separates review failure from actual rework and reclaims judgment work` | Integration | PASS |
| 4 | The same worker receives at most one mechanical rework | `allows one explicit mechanical rework and then forces controller recovery` | Integration | PASS |
| 5 | v0.3 `返工` records migrate to stopped `待决` records | `v0.3 changes-requested titles migrate safely to a stopped pending decision` | Migration | PASS |
| 6 | The public CLI drives registration through controller reclaim with the new required evidence | `CLI enforces readiness and drives an explicit controller reclaim flow` | CLI integration | PASS |
| 7 | `repeatable` accepts only `gpt-5.6-luna` and `bounded_reasoning` accepts only `gpt-5.6-terra` | `binds each delegated work class to its exact GPT-5.6 model` | Integration | PASS |
| 8 | Active legacy and mismatched registrations are reported without registry writes | `audits active legacy and mismatched model registrations without mutating them` | Audit integration | PASS |

## Coverage and known gaps

`node --experimental-test-coverage --test tests/task-control.test.mjs skill/codex-task-control/scripts/task-control.unit.test.mjs` reported 97.20% line coverage, 96.69% function coverage, and 71.64% branch coverage for `task-control.mjs`. Remaining branches are primarily bounded filesystem-error recovery and CLI invalid-argument paths; core lifecycle, routing, migration, lock ownership, and live-ledger isolation paths are covered.

## Merge evidence

- RED checkpoint: `6a62021 test: add red cases for routing readiness and rework state`
- GREEN checkpoint: `4ba863f feat: gate delegation and separate rework from review`
- Model-binding RED checkpoint: `416ef0d test: reproduce legacy model routing leak`
- Model-binding GREEN checkpoint: `cf9a557 fix: enforce GPT-5.6 worker model binding`
